This is a simple drafting task. No tools needed — no documents to reference, no search required. I should just write the email directly.

Subject: Request for Brief Extension — Contract Review Deadline

Dear [Counsel],

I hope this message finds you well. I'm writing to request a short extension of our current contract-review deadline by one week. A few scheduling items on my end have tightened my availability, and I want to make sure I can give the document the careful attention it deserves rather than rush the review.

I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause, and I'm happy to discuss a revised timeline that works for both sides. Please let me know if a one-week extension is workable, or if you'd prefer to propose an alternate date.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
